---
title: "Guilford Course Notes"
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)

note_entries <- c("Title", "Author", "Subject", "Pages", "University", "Abstract")

note_data <- c("Geometry In Practice and Numbers In Theory", "Hilton, Peter; Pedersen, Jean", "geometry", "41", "State University of New York at Binghamton (PH) and Santa Clara University (JP)", "The authors have for some years been studying the number theory which arises from a certain paper-folding algorithm. The work originated when one of the authors (JP) became involved in a local campaign in Northern California to reinvigorate the teaching of geometry. The plan which she conceived was to demonstrate a systematic procedure for folding paper strips to construct arbitrarily good approximations to certain regular convex polygons, and also to use the folded straight strips to construct interesting polyhedra. The campaign flourished -- and the mathematics became increasingly intriguing. This guide discusses the algorithms to construct star polygons, and generalizations.")


tibble(note_entries, note_data) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_align(align = "left") |>
  tab_style(
    style= cell_text(v_align = "top"),
    locations = cells_body()
  ) |>
  tab_options( table.width = "80%", column_labels.hidden = TRUE)  


```
- [Download File](./geometry-in-practice-and-numbers-in-theory_hilton_pedersen.pdf)

This course guide was originally published by the Guilford College Journal of Undergraduate Mathematics as a volume in the Monographs on Undergraduate Mathematics series.

This course guide may be reproduced and distributed if the following conditions are met:

- State that it is the property of the Math Department of Guilford College, and is protected under copyright by Guilford.
- Take no profit from its distribution. (You may recover copying costs if you are using these for a class.)
